member.name: Emma Foody
member.synopsis: Emma Foody is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Cramlington and Killingworth , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. She currently holds the Government post of Assistant Whip.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Medical Treatments: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many Individual Funding Requests have been made per Integrated Care Board over the last ten years, and how many of these have been approved.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The majority of individual funding requests (IFR) are actioned by integrated care boards, and there is no central database recording these requests.NHS England’s IFR Policy provides the framework through which requests outside routinely commissioned arran...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: A19: Freight
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made on the value of road freight journeys through Moor Farm roundabout in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the consideration of potential improvements to Moor Farm junction, analysis underpinning value for money assessments will look at the value of delays to road freight, with Transport Analysis Guidance being updated to reflect the latest position...

writtenQuestions.heading: National Highways: North of England
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many holding objections have been issued by National Highways on applications in the North East of England due to the potential impact on Moor Farm roundabout.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Highways currently has four planning applications on holding recommendations relating specifically to Moor Farm Junction on the A19.

writtenQuestions.heading: Veterans
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to improve support for veterans.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Remembrance is a poignant time for our veterans, allowing the country to demonstrate their recognition and celebrate the Service of our veterans. This Government has already taken swift action to deliver for our veterans, and cohere the support available....

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Recruitment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many and what proportion of recruits did not pass out of basic training in each service in each of the last five years.

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads and Roundabouts: Northumberland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, pursuant to the Answer of 16 June 2025 to Question 59057 on Roads and Roundabouts: Northumberland, how many direct representations her Department has received from (a) officials and (b) elected representatives
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Transport has received no formal representations from officials or elected representatives at Northumberland County Council on the Road Investment Strategy, Moor Farm Roundabout or Seaton Burn roundabout in the last 12 months. However i...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads and Roundabouts: Northumberland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what representations she has received from Northumberland County Council on (a) the Road Investment Strategy, (b) Moor Farm roundabout and (c) Seaton Burn roundabout in the last 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Officials from my Department and National Highways met with the North East Combined Authority, of which Northumberland County Council is a member, in January 2025 to discuss these and other matters. National Highways will continue to engage key local stak...

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing Estates: Reform
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps her Department is taking to reform estate adoption.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is determined to end the injustice of ‘fleecehold’ entirely and we will consult this year on legislative and policy options to reduce the prevalence of private estate management arrangements, which are the root cause of the problems experie...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: A19: Northumberland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what information her Department holds on levels of air pollution at Moor Farm Roundabout between the A19, A1 and A189 in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Highways does not have any air quality monitoring for Moor Farm Roundabout and has not monitored here, or similar locations, because there are no sensitive receptors such as houses or schools anywhere nearby.
